channel.title: Chroniq Now: Breaking Global News and Headlines Daily
channel.description: Chroniq Now - your trusted source for breaking global news, in-depth analysis and up-to-the-minute coverage of politics, business, culture and more.

item.title: Senate GOP Blocks Bid to End Iran War; New Push for Presidential Fitness Commission
item.description:
The U.S. Senate narrowly rejected a resolution to halt the conflict with Iran 47-52, while Rep. Jamie Raskin introduced a 25th Amendment bill to evaluate presidential capacity.

Key points:
• The 47-52 vote marks the fourth time this year the Senate has rejected a war powers resolution on Iran, with Sen. Rand Paul the only Republican in favor and Sen. John Fetterman the only Democrat opposed.
• Democrats argue the war is unconstitutional, as Congress never authorized it; the 60-day War Powers Act deadline expires at the end of April, raising the stakes for a potential House vote expected this week.
• Rep. Jamie Raskin introduced the Commission on Presidential Capacity Act, backed by 50 Democratic co-sponsors, which would create a bipartisan 17-member panel of physicians and former officials to assess whether the president is mentally or physically fit for office.
• The bill is considered a long shot, as it would need to pass a Republican-controlled Congress and could face a presidential veto; the White House dismissed the effort as politically motivated.

Read the full article: Senate GOP Blocks Bid to End Iran War; New Push for Presidential Fitness Commission

item.title: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
item.description:
Driven Advisors and Administrative Services face scrutiny in a legal dispute with Bancrédito Holding Corp, owner of Bancrédito International Bank and Trust.

Read the full article: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
